text: Please don't ever do this.
There seems to be this trend online of
people going to glaciers, specifically
in Alaska, and drinking the water. And
just because it looks like blue Gatorade
doesn't necessarily mean that it's safe
to drink. On one hand, you have tons of
people saying that glacier water is full
of ancient bacteria, animal feces, ice
worms, and more. And under no
circumstances should you ever drink it.
Then you have people like Ludicrous who
went and drank from a glacier in Alaska
and said it felt like every single one
of the cells in his body was being
hydrated at once.
Oh my god. Oh.
Professor Martin Truffer, who studies
glaciers for a living at the University
of Alaska, says that glacier water is
extremely clean and doesn't really pose
a health concern. But official
government health websites do not
recommend drinking raw glacier water and
say it should always be treated before
consumption.
